instructions.summary: Implement buffer pool and io_uring registered buffers
instructions.objective: Create a buffer pooling system supporting: userspace pools, io_uring fixed buffers (5.1+), and provided buffer rings (5.19+). Fallback gracefully on older kernels.
instructions.constraints: - Zero-allocation fast path - Kernel feature detection at runtime - Fallback chain: provided → fixed → pooled → allocated - Thread-safe for thread-per-core model - Integrate with connection.rs read paths

context.tree: harrow-server-monoio/ ├── src/ │ ├── lib.rs │ ├── connection.rs # Contains read_headers(), read_body() │ └── codec.rs └── tests/ └── integration.rs

guidance.notes:
Create new file: harrow-server-monoio/src/buffer.rs
Key structures: - BufferPool: userspace slab allocator - FixedBufferPool: IORING_REGISTER_BUFFERS - BufferRing: IORING_SETUP_BUF_RING (kernel 5.19+)
Integration points in connection.rs: - Line 53: BytesMut::with_capacity(8192) → pool.acquire() - Line 173: read buffer allocation → pool.get_read_buf()
Kernel detection: check /proc/sys/kernel/osrelease or runtime probe
